title: uinstopr
text: uinstopr Return the handle to an operation. Explain this code long uinstopr (UHACT hInst, UCHAR* oprName, int reserved, UHACT* phOpr ); Copy Parameters hInst The instance handle as returned by unewinst oprName The name of the operation to instantiate reserved This parameter is reserved for future use and is currently not used. We recommend you supply zero. phOpr (Output) The operation handle Return Values For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description uinstopr returns the handle of the specified operation in an existing component instance. The handle is returned in the parameter phOpr . The operation handle can subsequently be used to get or set parameters, and to activate the operation using uopract . Note that there is no function to delete an operation. However, you do need to delete the operation handle after use by calling ufreeh . Example: uecreate, uedelete, ufreeh, uinstdel, uinstnew, uinstopr, and uopract The following simple call-in program shows how to activate the EXEC operation of the service MYSVC. As usual, error checking has been omitted for the sake of brevity. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> void main (void) { UHACT hEnv; /* Uniface environment */ UHACT hIn; /* Instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handle */ long opStat; /* Activate status return parameter */ /* Create the environment */ uecreate(1, 0, 0, "my.asn", 0, 0, &hEnv); /* Instantiate service MYSVC */ uinstnew(hEnv, "MYSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, 0); uinstopr(hIn, "EXEC", 0, &hOp); /* Instantiate the EXEC operation */ uopract (hOp, &opStat, 0); /* Activate the operation */ uinstdel(hIn, 0); /* Destroy the instance */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free the operation */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free the instance */ uedelete(hEnv, -1); /* Shut down the environment */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free the environment handle */ return 0; // on success } Copy Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise.
